project_title: 13 snapshot workbooks; 11-phase build scaffold - AI-assisted methods
product_name: 13 snapshot workbooks; 11-phase build scaffold - AI-assisted methods
final_object_type: analytic method or human-AI evaluation module
runtime_target: structured public page plus project-specific data packet and proof runner
operational_end_state: A user can apply a method, compare baseline and method-supported reasoning, record human judgment, and preserve the claim boundary.
minimum_operational_release: A chapter or manuscript release slice for 13 snapshot workbooks; 11-phase build scaffold - AI-assisted methods: outline, argument state, source/passages used, revision rules, public title logic, and next reader-facing output.
full_build_definition: A manuscript system with chapter or section structure, source-governed argument logic, revision state, passage targets, reader-facing output, and publication-readiness checks.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 6 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
public_claim_now: May claim a prototype-to-full-build path exists; do not claim completed operational system.
current_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
blocking_gates: live capture and outside-use evidence before deployment or validation claims
first_full_build: Add promotion-router examples showing how a packet becomes a room, paper, software instrument, source reservoir, archive item, or blocked record.
launch_or_review_path: Open the manuscript/project contract for `mb-ic-analytic-technique-compendium-bundle-v0-1-v1-3`, select one chapter or section, and produce a source-bound release slice.

acceptance_tests: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence
source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation
privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
promotion test: item cannot advance without identity, authority, route, and next action
